description: Design system for personal content (Lim Chun Zhe). Shares structural patterns with corvia brand but uses its own color palette.

personal.name: Lim Chun Zhe
personal.description: Developer journal, discovery-style content. Dark theme with multi-color accents.

corvia.name: corvia
corvia.description: Product brand. Professional, restrained. Navy + gold. See repos/corvia/docs/brand/ for full guide.

shared.description: Structural patterns shared across both brands. Same layout rules, typography scale, and component patterns — only colors differ.

typography.hierarchyRule: Maintain >= 1.3x ratio between adjacent levels. Never adjust all sizes at once.

slideTemplates._description: Canonical slide types for LinkedIn carousels. AI should compose slides from these templates rather than generating arbitrary layouts. Each template defines its structure, content constraints, and height budget within the 936x1110px safe area.

cover.description: First slide. Hook the reader. Large title, subtitle, topic tags.
rules: Title should be max 8-12 words, split across 3-4 lines for impact
Use highlight class on the key phrase (1 color only)
Subtitle is 1-2 sentences, secondary color
Tags are pill-shaped, accent background at 15% opacity
No cards, no stat boxes, no diagrams — keep it clean

setup.description: Context slide. What was being built, what tools were used. Establishes credibility.
rules: Card row: icon (36px) + heading (22px) + description (18px, 2 lines max)
Each card has a distinct accent color on top border
Info cards use card-header (18px uppercase) + body (24px)
Total content must fit within ~780px after title+subtitle

comparison.description: Side-by-side broken vs correct. Show the problem visually.
rules: Left side: red border, cross emoji label, shows the broken version
Right side: green border, check emoji label, shows the correct version
Mock labels use mock-label class (16px uppercase)
Keep mockup content simple — just enough to show the problem
Max 2 comparison pairs per slide. If more, split into multiple slides
Flex children MUST have min-width: 0 to prevent overflow
Insight box at bottom ties the comparison together

category-grid.description: Show 4 categories/types in a 2x2 grid with supporting stats.
rules: Cards: emoji (36px) + heading (22px bold) + description (18px secondary, 2 lines)
Each card has a distinct accent color on left border
Stat row: stat-value (48px bold, colored) + stat-label (18px secondary)
Use grid-template-columns, NOT flex, for stat row
If including both quote-card and stat-row, reduce card padding to 24px

diagram.description: Visual explanation with flow diagrams or process comparisons.
rules: Flow steps: colored pill boxes (20px) connected by arrows (28px)
Use flex-direction: column for vertical flows within each column
Gap between steps: 14px
Step pills: accent bg at 15% opacity, 1px accent border, border-radius 12px, padding 16px 28px
Width: 90% of column to leave breathing room
Label below flow (24px, colored) summarizes the outcome
Never use absolute positioning for flow steps

solution.description: Present the fix. Pipeline diagram + code snippet + summary.
rules: Pipeline steps: icon + label (20px bold) in accent-colored pills
Arrows between steps: 32px, accent-green
Terminal/code block: JetBrains Mono (20px), dark background (#0d1117)
Keep code to essential config only — no boilerplate
Summary card: border-left accent-green, body at 24px

before-after.description: Two-column comparison of workflow/results before and after the solution.
rules: Left card: red top border, icon (36px), red heading (22px), bullet list (20px, 5 items max)
Right card: green top border, icon (36px), green heading (22px), bullet list (20px, 5 items max)
Bullet lists use secondary color, line-height 1.8
Related topics: small colored cards with label (20px bold) + description (18px)
Insight box ties together the 'so what' message

takeaway.description: Final slide. Key lessons + CTA. Use closing gradient background.
rules: Each takeaway: emoji icon (32px) + bold heading + secondary description (2 lines)
Takeaway text: 24px primary weight for heading, 24px secondary color for description
Max 4 takeaways — if more, the slide gets too dense
CTA: question (24px semibold secondary) + invitation (20px muted)
End with engagement hook — open question, not a statement

stat-highlight.description: Big numbers that tell a story. 2-4 stats with context.
rules: Stat boxes: value (48px bold colored) + label (18px secondary)
Use grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr — never flex for stat layout
Each stat a different accent color for visual variety
2 stats = 1 row, 3-4 stats = 2 rows. Never 3 columns (too cramped at 936px)
Stat values should be short: numbers, percentages, or 1-2 word labels
Context card explains what the numbers mean

narrativeArc._description: Recommended slide sequence for a discovery-style LinkedIn carousel (6-10 slides).

structure.purpose: The pattern — why it keeps happening

guidelines: 6-8 slides is the sweet spot. Under 5 feels thin, over 10 loses attention.
Every slide must earn its place — if it doesn't add new information, cut it.
Alternate between text-heavy and visual slides for rhythm.
The 'discovery journey' tone: honest about failures, specific about what you tried, generous with what you learned.

contentStrategy._description: How slides and post description work together. Slides are the primary content vehicle; the post guides readers through the arc.

principles: Slides MUST tell the complete story standalone. Many readers never read the post description.
Each slide must have enough context to be understood without the previous slide or the post text.
Include 'what is this project?' context on the cover slide — don't assume the reader knows.
Use subtitles and insight boxes to add the context that would otherwise be in the post.
Jargon must be explained on the slide where it first appears (e.g., 'OpenVINO EP' → 'hardware backend').
Diagrams and visuals carry better than paragraphs. Prefer flow charts, split layouts, and stat boxes over blocks of text.
Slides should have a visual:text ratio that favors visual. If a slide looks like a wall of text, redesign it.
The post description is a SHORT narrative arc that guides people through the slides, not a standalone essay.
Target 6-8 short paragraphs. Each paragraph maps roughly to 1-2 slides.
Hook → Problem → Solution → Key insight → Messy part (1 sentence, 'slide N has the breakdown') → Proud moment → Dogfooding/meta → CTA.
Reference specific slides when detail lives there: '(Slide 5 has the full breakdown.)'
Keep technical detail light — enough to intrigue, not enough to explain. The slides do the explaining.
Tone: down to earth, developer journey, honest. Not a press release, not a tutorial.

tone.voice: Developer journal — first person, honest, specific
do: Be specific about what went wrong
